statement: Adult human body density is near-constant at approximately 1010 kg/m^3, varying by only about 2-3% across individuals (slightly less for muscular, slightly more for lean build). Water is 1000 kg/m^3; fat is slightly less dense; bone and muscle slightly more.
source: physiology; Shaw & Costill, Physiology of Sport and Exercise (widely cited)

statement: Adult human body volume scales approximately with the cube of height: volume_m3 ≈ k · height_cm^3, where k ≈ 1.4e-8 m^3/cm^3. This is the geometric consequence of isometric scaling (similar body shapes at different sizes).
source: derived from anthropometric norms (70 kg / 1010 kg/m^3 ≈ 0.069 m^3 at 170 cm)

statement: Weight scales approximately as the cube of height: weight_kg ≈ theta · height_cm^3 + noise, with theta ≈ 1.414e-5 kg/cm^3 (= body_density × volume_coefficient). This is derivable from the two priors above and agrees with adult population data: 170 cm → ~70 kg, 150 cm → ~48 kg, 190 cm → ~97 kg.
cross_domain_isomorph: This cube-law form recurs in: tree biomass vs trunk diameter, fish mass vs length, planet mass vs radius (at fixed density). Same mathematical structure as any density × volume composition.

statement: BMI = weight_kg / height_m^2. Adult population mean BMI ≈ 22-25, with std ≈ 4. Note BMI uses height² not height³ — an empirical fit to the observed narrower relationship between weight and height at the population (not individual) level.
confidence: high for population, low for individual prediction

statement: Adult human heights (combined sexes, developed nations) are approximately Normal-distributed with mean 170 cm and std 10 cm. Adult males mean ~177, adult females mean ~163.
Adult human weights are approximately Normal-distributed with mean ~70 kg and std ~12 kg, with a slight right skew. Correlated with height (corr ≈ 0.6).
European shoe size (EU) relates to foot length by shoe_size_eu ≈ 1.5 · foot_length_cm + 2 (approximate; different manufacturers vary). Adult foot length ~22-29 cm → EU 35-45.

Sole contact area for an adult foot, when standing barefoot, is roughly 0.5 · foot_length^2 × aspect_ratio, giving typical values of 140-180 cm^2 for adult feet. Rough rule: foot_area_cm2 ≈ 0.3 × foot_length_cm^2 (moderate-precision anthropometric estimate).
source: anthropometric tables (average adult sole print area)

statement: Pressure = Force / Area. For a person standing evenly on both feet: pressure_pa = (weight_kg · 9.81) / foot_area_total_m^2. Typical adult standing pressure ~20-30 kPa per foot; walking ~2x (one foot contact).

Footprint indentation depth in soft ground scales inversely with soil stiffness and linearly with applied pressure (in the linear-elastic / Winkler regime): footprint_depth_cm ≈ pressure_pa / soil_stiffness_pa_per_cm. Typical wet sand stiffness ~1e5 Pa/cm (varies 2-3x with moisture).
source: Winkler foundation model; approximate for deformable substrates
cross_domain_isomorph: Same linear-response structure as Ohm's law (V = I·R), spring deformation (F = k·x), or viscous flow — input ÷ stiffness = response.
